mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll momentum signals expanding household income and lower near-term recession risk; higher PAYEMS growth has a positive causal effect on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through expected earnings upgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:2015-10 PAYEMS vintage value was 142654 thousand
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month growth coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests or if triggered positive payroll momentum months produce 20-day SPX returns no higher than baseline at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds liftoff from near zero raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its prior 12-month maximum and rises >0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2015-10 FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.12 percent
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of liftoff-triggered observations or if the FEDFUNDS change coefficient is nonnegative in rolling 3-year walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with a low and stable unemployment rate identifies resilient consumer demand; this joint condition raises expected corporate revenues and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth >0 and UNRATE <=5.5% with UNRATE not rising over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2015-10 RSAFS vintage value was 447255 million dollars
fact:2015-10 UNRATE vintage value was 5.0 percent
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with tight but stable labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-by-UNRATE interaction term is not positive or if the joint trigger produces 20-day SPX returns below the unconditional mean in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and operating leverage pressure, lowering expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: industrial slowdown or manufacturing recession scare
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if downside hit rate is not above the unconditional negative-return rate.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above trend indicate improving construction demand and durable-goods spillovers, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its trailing 12-month average by >5%
evidence_refs: fact:2015-09 HOUST vintage value was 1206 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST above-trend triggers have a nonpositive average 60-day SPX return or if the HOUST signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with contained inflation keep real discount rates low and reduce the probability of immediate tightening, supporting risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2015-10 FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.12 percent
regime_hint: zero lower bound with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS-by-CPIAUCSL low-rate low-inflation interaction is not positive or if 5-day returns under the joint trigger are not above baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Very low unemployment combined with accelerating consumer prices signals overheating and a higher probability of monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE <=5.0% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:2015-10 UNRATE vintage value was 5.0 percent
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE high-CPI trigger is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive real GDP reading paired with flat or falling retail sales identifies stale aggregate growth against weakening current demand; this divergence predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarter annualized growth >2% while RSAFS 3-month growth <=0
evidence_refs: fact:2015-07 GDPC1 vintage value was 16394.2 billions of chained 2017 dollars
fact:2015-10 RSAFS vintage value was 447255 million dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-positive and RSAFS-weak divergence does not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns or if the interaction term fails to be negative in rolling validation.
